figures.caption: Table 1: The four splits of AIPsy-Affect.

Figure 1: Three-method NLP defense battery applied to AIPsy-Affect. Left: VADER compound sentiment by split. Middle: NRC negative-emotion word fraction by split. Right: GoEmotions DistilRoBERTa P (emotional) by split. Set A (keyword-rich, sourced from crowd-enVENT) is detected by all three methods; the keyword-free clinical split is detected as more emotional than its matched neutral controls only by the contextual classifier.

Figure 2: GoEmotions DistilRoBERTa class probabilities aggregated by target Plutchik emotion on the keyword-free clinical split. Probability mass collapses onto go_disgust across most target emotions, indicating a generic “something disturbing” response rather than categoryspecific recognition. Top-1 accuracy is 5.2% (chance = 14.3%; 82.5% on the keyword-rich Set A control).
